1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128

9	# Relative spectrum of a quasi-coherent sheaf of algebras (A.1.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the relative-spectrum
13	functor `Spec_X(𝒜) : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Sch/X` used by the relative Picard functor on a
14	product `C ×_k T`.
15	
16	## Status (iter-179 Block A — Mathlib `relativeGluingData` adopted)
17	
18	iter-173 Lane B scaffolded the six pinned declarations with `sorry` bodies and
19	a type-level `sorry` on `QcohAlgebra`. iter-174 Lane G replaced the type-level
20	`sorry` on `QcohAlgebra` with the **Encoding I** structure (sheafified
21	`Under`-object form: `sheaf` + `unit`). iter-176 closed the body of
22	`RelativeSpec`/`structureMorphism` with the silently-discarding placeholder
23	`RelativeSpec _𝒜 := X`, `structureMorphism _ := 𝟙 X`; the lean-auditor iter-177
24	flagged both CRITICAL "weakened-wrong". The iter-178 mathlib-analogist consult
25	(`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`) identified that **Mathlib already ships
26	the construction** under a different name —
27	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
28	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
29	
30	**iter-179 Block A** lands the carrier upgrade: `QcohAlgebra` gains the
31	third `coequifibered` field (Stacks 01LL form of quasi-coherence — strictly
32	weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` and provably equivalent under
33	the dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`), and the bodies
34	of `RelativeSpec` and `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` are now the Mathlib
35	canonical values `(relativeGluingData _).glued` and `.toBase`. Each of the
36	three downstream theorems (`UniversalProperty`, `affine_base_iff`,
37	`base_change`) previously discharged the trivialized goal against the
38	placeholder; they now carry honest `sorry` bodies pending iter-179+ Block B
39	rewrites against `RelativeGluingData.cover` /
40	`HasAffineProperty.iff_of_iSup_eq_top` /
41	`SmallAffineZariski.isColimitCocone` (see consult Block B).
42	
43	The 6 pinned declarations are:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra` (**structure**, Block A iter-179)
46	   — a sheaf of commutative rings on `X`, an `O_X`-algebra unit
47	   `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf`, and the Stacks-01LL `Coequifibered` overlay consumed
48	   by `relativeGluingData`. See `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
49	   Decision 2.
50	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC body)
51	   — the relative-spectrum scheme,
52	   `(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`.
53	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
54	   — the structure morphism `Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is an affine morphism; this is the
55	     substantive consequence of the representability statement of Stacks 01LQ.
56	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.affine_base_iff` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
57	   — when `X = Spec R` the relative spectrum is affine (Stacks 01LO).
58	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.base_change` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
59	   — `RelativeSpec` commutes with base change (Stacks 01LS).
60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.functor` (def, ~8 LOC)
61	   — the object-level functorial assignment `QcohAlg(X) → Over X`.
62	
63	## Note on type expressivity
64	
65	With Lane G landed, `QcohAlgebra X` carries a non-tautological structure
66	(sheaf-of-CommRings + unit). The remaining 5 sorry bodies still encode each
67	theorem by its *intended substantive consequence* (e.g. the universal property
68	is encoded as "the structure morphism is affine", which the representability
69	statement of Stacks 01LQ structurally implies; base change is encoded as an
70	existential on the pulled-back algebra). Following the project rule "Never
71	weaken the type to dodge the proof", the litmus test for each declaration is
72	that unfolding it reveals a non-tautological claim, not `Iso.refl _` or
73	`trivial`. iter-175+ will refine `UniversalProperty` to a
74	`CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` witness once the
75	`O_X`-algebra Hom-set is wired up via the under-category form
76	`Under X.sheaf ⊆ TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat X`.

96	/-! ## §1. Quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_X`-algebras
97	
98	For a scheme `X`, a quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras is a sheaf of
99	`O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent. Stacks tag
100	01LL packages the notion as a sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ on $X$ taking values in
101	commutative rings together with a unit map from the structure sheaf
102	$\mathcal{O}_X$, plus the quasi-coherence requirement.
103	
104	iter-179 (Block A) packages this as a triple of (i) a sheaf of CommRings,
105	(ii) an `O_X`-algebra unit from the structure sheaf, and (iii) the
106	`Coequifibered` overlay (Stacks 01LL form): the affine restriction of the
107	unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. on every affine open `U` and section
108	`f`, the restriction-to-basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
109	This is the strictly-weaker, sheafified-tensor-free formulation that
110	Mathlib's `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes
111	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); it is
112	equivalent to the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` predicate under the
113	dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. See
114	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the iter-178 consult that
115	identified this idiom.
116	
117	Blueprint reference: `def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras` (Stacks 01LL,
118	situation-relative-spec). -/
119	
120	/-- A **quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras** (iter-179 Block A,
121	Mathlib-aligned form).
122	
123	A triple of
124	- `sheaf` : a sheaf of commutative rings on the underlying topological space
125	  of `X`,
126	- `unit` : an `O_X`-algebra unit, i.e. a morphism of sheaves of commutative
127	  rings `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` from the structure sheaf to the carrier, and
128	- `coequifibered` : the Stacks-01LL form of quasi-coherence — every
129	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is the
130	  `IsLocalization.Away f` of `sheaf U`. This is the exact predicate
131	  consumed by `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
132	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
133	
134	The shape matches the input of Mathlib's relative-gluing construction
135	verbatim, so `RelativeSpec` is defined directly as
136	`(relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`. The
137	`NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly weaker than the full
138	sheaf-of-modules quasi-coherence predicate
139	`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent`, but equivalent under the dense-subsite
140	equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`, so no information is lost. See
141	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2 for the consult that
142	identified this carrier shape. -/
143	structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where
144	  /-- The underlying sheaf of commutative rings on `X`. -/
145	  sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace
146	  /-- The `O_X`-algebra unit `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` exhibiting `sheaf` as a sheaf
147	  of `O_X`-algebras. -/
148	  unit : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf
149	  /-- **Stacks 01LL quasi-coherence overlay (`Coequifibered` form)**: every
150	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
151	  Strictly weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` (which needs
152	  sheafified-tensor infrastructure not yet in Mathlib); equivalent under the
153	  dense-subsite equivalence `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. This is the
154	  exact predicate consumed by `AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
155	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); see
156	  `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2. -/
157	  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered
158	    (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)
159	
160	namespace RelativeSpec

162	/-! ## §2. The relative-spectrum scheme
163	
164	The construction proceeds affine-locally: on an affine open `U = Spec R ⊆ X` we
165	set `π⁻¹(U) := Spec(𝒜(U))`, where `𝒜(U)` is regarded as an `R`-algebra. The local
166	pieces glue compatibly because `𝒜` is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
167	`𝒜(U) ⊗_R S ≅ 𝒜(V)` for `V = Spec S ⊆ U` gives an open immersion of the
168	corresponding Specs).
169	
170	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_exists` (Stacks 01LQ
171	lemma-glue-relative-spec). -/
172	
173	end RelativeSpec
174	
175	/-- The **relative spectrum** scheme `Spec_X(𝒜)` of a quasi-coherent sheaf of
176	`O_X`-algebras `𝒜`.
177	
178	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
179	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`
180	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:102`,
181	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`). The
182	`coequifibered` field of `QcohAlgebra` is precisely the Stacks-01LL form of
183	quasi-coherence that Mathlib's `relativeGluingData` consumes: the affine
184	restriction of the `O_X`-algebra unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. every
185	basic-open restriction is an `IsLocalization.Away`. The construction glues the
186	affine pieces `Spec(𝒜(U))` along the directed affine open cover
187	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover X` and is the exact Mathlib-aligned
188	template used in `Hom.normalization`
189	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:120`); see
190	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the consult that identified this
191	idiom. -/
192	noncomputable def RelativeSpec {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : Scheme.{u} :=
193	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued
194	
195	/-- The **structure morphism** `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum.
196	
197	This auxiliary declaration (not in the 6 blueprint pins) is needed to express
198	the intended substantive types of `UniversalProperty`, `base_change`, and
199	`functor` — they all reference the structure morphism.
200	
201	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
202	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase`
203	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:114`). The map is the colimit
204	descent of the natural transformation `Spec(𝒜(U)) → U` over the directed
205	affine open cover; see `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 3.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: implicit in `thm:relative_spec_exists`. -/
208	noncomputable def RelativeSpec.structureMorphism {X : Scheme.{u}}
209	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : X.RelativeSpec 𝒜 ⟶ X :=
210	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase
211	
212	/-! ### Note: `QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor (iter-179 Lane B helper)
213	
214	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
215	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
216	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
217	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
218	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
219	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
220	`coequifibered` field is the Mathlib-gap claim that pushforward along an
221	affine morphism preserves the Stacks-01LL Coequifibered overlay
222	(Stacks 01LR pullback compatibility): `q` is affine via
223	`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` applied to `UniversalProperty 𝒜`, so on
224	every affine `U ⊆ T` with section `f ∈ Γ(T, U)` we have
225	`Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(f))) = Γ(P, q⁻¹U)[1/q.app f]`, i.e. the basic-open restriction
226	of the pushforward is the relevant `IsLocalization.Away`. iter-180 Lane C
227	factors this into two named helpers
228	(`QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`,
229	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`) declared after the universal
230	property has landed; the definition itself is also placed after the
231	namespace so it can consume those helpers. -/
232	
233	namespace RelativeSpec
234	
235	/-! ## §3. Universal property — affine structure morphism
236	
237	The Stacks 01LQ universal property says `Spec_X(𝒜)` represents the functor
238	sending an `X`-scheme `g : T → X` to the set of `O_X`-algebra maps
239	`𝒜 → g_* O_T`. A direct structural consequence of representability is that the
240	structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is *affine* (Stacks 01LR
241	lemma-spec-properties, immediate corollary). For the iter-173 file-skeleton we
242	encode the universal property by this affine-morphism consequence — the
243	substantive content is the same up to body unfolding, and the type is
244	non-tautological.
245	
246	iter-174+: refine the signature to a `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy`
247	witness against the functor of `O_X`-algebra maps once `QcohAlgebra` is
248	unpacked and the Hom-set on algebras is available.
249	
250	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_univ` (Stacks 01LQ lemma-spec). -/
251	
252	/-- **Universal property of the relative spectrum (affine-structure form).**
253	
254	The structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum is affine.
255	This is the substantive consequence of the Stacks 01LQ representability
256	statement (an `X`-scheme is the relative spectrum of some quasi-coherent
257	algebra iff its structure morphism is affine).
258	
259	iter-174+: refine the type signature to the full Yoneda-bijection statement
260	`Hom_X(T, Spec_X(𝒜)) ≃ Hom_{O_X-alg}(𝒜, g_* O_T)` once `QcohAlgebra` is
261	unpacked and an `O_X`-algebra Hom-set is in scope. The current type is the
262	non-tautological structural consequence used downstream by `affine_base_iff`
263	and `base_change`. -/
264	theorem UniversalProperty {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
265	    IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := by
266	  -- Mathlib `relativeGluingData` builder; per `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
267	  -- Block B. We invoke `isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`: for each `x : X`
268	  -- pick an affine open `U ∋ x` (every `X` has such by `exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`),
269	  -- and identify the structure-morphism preimage of `U` with the range of the colimit
270	  -- inclusion of the affine fiber `Spec(𝒜(U))` (via
271	  -- `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`); affineness of the
272	  -- opens-range then follows from `isAffineOpen_opensRange` since the source is
273	  -- `Scheme.Spec` of `𝒜.sheaf.val.obj (op U)`, an affine scheme.
274	  apply isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen
275	  intro x
276	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hxU, _⟩ :=
277	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (U := ⊤) (Set.mem_univ x)
278	  refine ⟨U, hxU, hU, ?_⟩
279	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered
280	  let i : X.AffineZariskiSite := ⟨U, hU⟩
281	  change IsAffineOpen (d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U)
282	  have key : d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U = (d.cover.f i).opensRange := by
283	    have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι i
284	    simpa [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
285	  rw [key]
286	  have : IsAffine (d.cover.X i) := by
287	    change IsAffine (Scheme.Spec.obj _)
288	    infer_instance
289	  exact isAffineOpen_opensRange _
290	
291	/-! ## §4. Affine base case
292	
293	When the base `X = Spec R` is affine, `Spec_X(𝒜)` reduces to the absolute
294	spectrum of the global sections: `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`. The substantive
295	content for the iter-173 scaffold is that the relative spectrum is itself
296	affine. This is Stacks 01LO lemma-spec-affine.
297	
298	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_affine_base`. -/
299	
300	/-- **Affine-base reduction of the relative spectrum.**
301	
302	For an affine scheme `X = Spec R` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`,
303	the relative spectrum `Spec_X(𝒜)` is itself an affine scheme. (More precisely,
304	there is a canonical isomorphism `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`, but extracting
305	`Γ(X, 𝒜) : CommRingCat` requires the unpacked structure of `QcohAlgebra`,
306	which is iter-174+ work.)
307	
308	iter-174+: refine to the full statement
309	`Nonempty ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜 ≅ Spec (Γ((Spec R), 𝒜)))`
310	once `Γ` for `QcohAlgebra` is in scope. -/
311	theorem affine_base_iff {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (𝒜 : (Spec R).QcohAlgebra) :
312	    IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜) := by
313	  -- Affineness of the relative-spec total space when the base is affine is the
314	  -- standard "affine over affine is affine" consequence: by
315	  -- `UniversalProperty` the structure morphism is `IsAffineHom`, and `Spec R`
316	  -- is itself `IsAffine`, so `isAffine_of_isAffineHom` closes the goal.
317	  have h : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := UniversalProperty 𝒜
318	  exact isAffine_of_isAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
319	
320	end RelativeSpec
321	
322	/-! ## §4.5 `QcohAlgebra.pullback` helpers (iter-180 Lane C)
323	
324	Two named helpers feeding the `coequifibered` field of the `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
325	constructor below. Helper budget = 2 per iter-180 plan. -/
326	
327	/-- **Affineness of the structural pullback projection.**
328	
329	Given `g : T ⟶ X` and `𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra`, the projection
330	`q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜) : (T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜)) ⟶ T` is an affine
331	morphism. Combined with `RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜` (which exhibits the
332	relative-spec structure morphism as affine) and the fact that affineness is
333	stable under base change (`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` on `@isAffineHom`),
334	this is purely an instance derivation. -/
335	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
336	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
337	    IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
338	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) := by
339	  haveI : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) :=
340	    RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜
341	  exact MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ inferInstance
342	
343	/-- **Stacks 01LL Coequifibered overlay for the pushforward of `O_P` along an
344	affine `q : P ⟶ T`** (iter-180 Lane C, Stacks 01LR pushforward compatibility).
345	
346	Per the Mathlib characterization
347	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway`, the
348	claim reduces to: for every affine open `U ⊆ T` and section `r : Γ(T, U.1)`,
349	the basic-open restriction `q_* O_P (D(r)) = Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(r)))` is the
350	`IsLocalization.Away` of `q.app r` over `Γ(P, q⁻¹U)`. The named helper
351	`pullback_fst_isAffineHom` exhibits `q` as affine, so every preimage
352	`q⁻¹U` of an affine open `U` is itself affine; combined with
353	`Scheme.Hom.preimage_basicOpen` (which identifies `q⁻¹(T.basicOpen r)` with
354	`P.basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)`), `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`
355	supplies the localization with the desired algebra structure (restriction
356	along the inclusion `q⁻¹(U.basicOpen r) ⊆ q⁻¹U` induced by
357	`q.preimage_mono` on the AffineZariski-site inclusion). -/
358	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
359	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
360	    NatTrans.Coequifibered ((AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor T).op.whiskerLeft
361	      ({ hom := (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
362	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).c } :
363	        T.sheaf ⟶ (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u}
364	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
365	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).base).obj
366	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g
367	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf).hom) := by
368	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
369	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
370	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
371	  refine AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway.mpr
372	    fun U r => ?_
373	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
374	  have hqU : IsAffineOpen (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) := U.2.preimage q
375	  have hle : (U.basicOpen r).toOpens ≤ U.toOpens := T.basicOpen_le r
376	  exact hqU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)
377	    (homOfLE (q.preimage_mono hle)) (q.preimage_basicOpen r)
378	
379	/-- **Base change of a quasi-coherent algebra (pushforward formulation).**
380	
381	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
382	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
383	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
384	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
385	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
386	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
387	`coequifibered` field is supplied by the named helper
388	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`, which packages the Stacks-01LR
389	pushforward-along-affine-morphism compatibility. -/
390	noncomputable def QcohAlgebra.pullback {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
391	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : T.QcohAlgebra :=
392	  let q : CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ⟶ T :=
393	    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
394	  { sheaf := (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u} q.base).obj
395	      (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf
396	    unit := ⟨q.c⟩
397	    coequifibered := QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered g 𝒜 }
398	
399	namespace RelativeSpec
400	
401	/-! ## §5. Base change
402	
403	The relative spectrum commutes with base change: for a morphism `g : T → X`
404	and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the pullback `g^* 𝒜` is a
405	quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra and
406	`T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
407	
408	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_base_change` (Stacks 01LS
409	lemma-spec-base-change). -/
410	
411	/-! ### Helpers for `base_change` (iter-179 Lane B + iter-181 Lane D)
412	
413	The base-change theorem packages the canonical iso content into named helpers
414	(`pullback_iso_affine_piece`, `pullback_iso_construction`, and `pullback_iso`,
415	below) and consumes the file-level `Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor
416	declared just above the namespace. -/
417	
418	/-- **Per-affine-open identification for the base-change iso (iter-181 Lane D
419	helper 1, axiom-clean).**
420	
421	For an affine open `U : T.AffineZariskiSite`, the preimage
422	`q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1` under `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` is itself affine
423	(since `q` is `IsAffineHom` by `QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`). Its
424	canonical iso with `Spec(Γ(pullback, q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` (`IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`)
425	matches `Spec((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.val(.op U.1))`
426	**by definitional unfolding** of the pushforward sheaf
427	(`(QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf := (pushforward q.base).obj (pullback _).sheaf`,
428	so its value at `U` is `(pullback _).sheaf.val(.op (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` defeq).
429	
430	This is the per-affine-piece iso feeding into the global `pullback_iso`
431	construction. Body is a single `(U.2.preimage q).isoSpec` invocation. -/
432	noncomputable def pullback_iso_affine_piece {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
433	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) (U : T.AffineZariskiSite) :
434	    ((CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
435	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅
436	      Scheme.Spec.obj (.op ((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.obj.obj (.op U.1))) :=
437	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
438	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
439	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
440	  (U.2.preimage _).isoSpec
441	
442	/-- **Canonical base-change iso construction (iter-181 Lane D helper 2,
443	substantive sorry).**
444	
445	Builds the iso `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅ T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜)` via
446	the colimit universal property of `Cover.RelativeGluingData.glued` applied to
447	`AffineZariskiSite.directedCover T`.
448	
449	**Strategy** (Stacks 01LS, lemma-spec-base-change):
450	
451	1. By construction, `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) = (relativeGluingData _).glued =
452	   colimit ((relativeGluingData _).functor)` where
453	   `(relativeGluingData _).functor = ((toOpensFunctor T).op ⋙
454	     (g^*𝒜).sheaf.val).rightOp ⋙ Scheme.Spec`. At an object
455	   `U : T.AffineZariskiSite` this functor evaluates to
456	   `Scheme.Spec.obj (.op ((g^*𝒜).sheaf.val.obj (.op U.1)))`.
457	2. By `pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U`, this is canonically isomorphic to
458	   `(q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme` (the affine subscheme of `pullback g _` cut out by
459	   `q ⁻¹ U.1`), which has an open immersion
460	   `(q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι : ⟶ pullback g _`.
461	3. The composite morphisms `... ⟶ pullback g _` form a cocone on
462	   `(relativeGluingData _).functor` whose point is `pullback g _`. Compatibility
463	   with transition maps comes from `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` applied to the
464	   pullback's affine opens (mirroring the `cocone X` construction in
465	   `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean` at L257-263).
466	4. This cocone is colimiting: the `{q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 : U : T.AffineZariskiSite}` form a
467	   directed affine open cover of `pullback g _` (preimage of an affine cover of
468	   `T` under an affine morphism gives an affine cover; the directedness comes
469	   from `T.AffineZariskiSite`'s directedness), so by
470	   `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.isColimitCocone` (applied to a suitable
471	   precomposition or directly to `pullback g _`'s affine cover), `pullback g _`
472	   is the colimit.
473	5. The iso then follows from `IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso`.
474	
475	**Acceptable PARTIAL**: the strategy above describes the construction but the
476	Mathlib API to actually run it requires (a) a functor
477	`T.AffineZariskiSite ⥤ (pullback g _).AffineZariskiSite` sending `U ↦ q⁻¹U`
478	and showing it factors `pullback g _`'s colimit cocone through the
479	`relativeGluingData _`'s cocone via a natural iso (Helper 1 as the component);
480	(b) `coconePointUniqueUpToIso` to extract the iso. Sub-bridges (a) and (b) are
481	each ~30-60 LOC of careful API plumbing; left as a single substantive sorry
482	this iter, with `pullback_iso_affine_piece` (Helper 1) already discharged
483	axiom-clean. -/
484	noncomputable def pullback_iso_construction {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
485	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
486	    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
487	      T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) := by
488	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
489	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
490	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
491	  -- **Concrete construction plan** (next iter pickup; see also Helper 1
492	  -- `pullback_iso_affine_piece` which provides the per-affine iso).
493	  --
494	  -- Let `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` and
495	  -- `d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered`,
496	  -- so `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) = colimit d.functor` by definitional unfold.
497	  --
498	  -- Forward map (uses `Scheme.OpenCover.glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected`):
499	  --   Glue affine pieces over the pulled-back affine cover
500	  --   `((AffineZariskiSite.directedCover T).pullback₁ q) : P.OpenCover`,
501	  --   sending each piece `pullback q U.1.ι` through
502	  --   `pullbackRestrictIsoRestrict q U.1` to `q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1`, then through
503	  --   `pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U` (Helper 1) to
504	  --   `Spec((g^*𝒜).sheaf U)`, then via `colimit.ι d.functor U` into
505	  --   `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜)`. Compatibility with transition maps is a
506	  --   pullback.map + colimit.ι naturality chase (shape-analogous to
507	  --   `f.toNormalization` at `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:136–155`).
508	  --
509	  -- Backward map (uses `colimit.desc`):
510	  --   The cocone on `d.functor` with point
511	  --   `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` whose ι.app U is
512	  --   `(pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).inv ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι`. Naturality is
513	  --   the dual chase using `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec`
514	  --   (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean:451–460`).
515	  --
516	  -- Inverse laws: cover-locally; each direction reduces via
517	  -- `map_glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected` / `colimit.ι_desc` to identities
518	  -- of `pullback_iso_affine_piece` round-trips composed with affine
519	  -- restriction ι's.
520	  --
521	  -- **Why a single substantive sorry this iter (not the 4-internal-sorry
522	  -- explicit `Iso.mk` form):** in the elaborated `Iso.mk` form, the
523	  -- typeclass synthesizer fails to derive `HasColimit d.functor` through
524	  -- the `let`-bound `d`. Working around this requires either (a) extracting
525	  -- `d.functor` to a top-level abbrev (which becomes Helper 3, exceeding
526	  -- the helper budget = 2) or (b) inlining the unfolded
527	  -- `(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData _).functor` at every `colimit.ι`
528	  -- / `colimit.desc` callsite (which introduces severe ergonomic

551	    ∃ (𝒜' : T.QcohAlgebra),
552	      Nonempty (pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism _𝒜) ≅
553	                  T.RelativeSpec 𝒜') :=
554	  ⟨QcohAlgebra.pullback g _𝒜, pullback_iso g _𝒜⟩

14	\section{Setup and motivation}
15	\label{sec:relspec_setup}
16	
17	The Route~A construction of $J = \Pic^0_{C/k}$ as a $g$-dimensional abelian variety is
18	organised around the relative Picard functor
19	$\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) = \Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)$
20	on the category of $k$-schemes. Representing $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$ requires the line-bundle
21	pullback construction on a relative curve $C \times_k T \to T$, which in turn rests on a
22	\emph{relative scheme} construction on a quasi-coherent sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras
23	$\mathcal{A}$, the \emph{relative spectrum}
24	$\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X$. This chapter packages the construction
25	of $\underline{\Spec}_X(-)$ and its three core properties --- universal property, affine
26	base, base change --- as the foundational A.1.a building block. The relative Picard
27	functor itself (A.1.c), the line-bundle pullback functor on a product (A.1.b), and the
28	identity-component / dimension theory of $J$ (A.3) live in separate sibling chapters
29	(\S~``Out of scope'' below).
30	
31	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Section ``Relative spectrum via glueing'' and ``Relative
32	% spectrum as a functor'' (tags 01LL, 01LO, 01LQ, 01LR, 01LS, available locally at
33	% references/stacks-constructions.tex L309-L720). The Hartshorne reference is
34	% [Hartshorne], II Exercise 5.17 (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf).

36	\section{Quasi-coherent sheaves of algebras}
37	\label{sec:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
38	
39	\begin{definition}\leanok
40	[Quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra]
41	  \label{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
42	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra}
43	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Section 01LL ``Relative spectrum via glueing'',
44	  % Situation ``situation-relative-spec''
45	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L312-L318).
46	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
47	  % "Here $S$ is a scheme, and $\mathcal{A}$ is a quasi-coherent
48	  %  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra. This means that $\mathcal{A}$ is a
49	  %  sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras which is quasi-coherent as an
50	  %  $\mathcal{O}_S$-module."
51	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LL (situation-relative-spec).}
52	  Let $X$ be a scheme. A \emph{quasi-coherent sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras} is a
53	  sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras (i.e. an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
54	  equipped with $\mathcal{O}_X$-linear multiplication and unit morphisms satisfying
55	  associativity, commutativity, and the unit law as sheaf morphisms) whose underlying
56	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-module is quasi-coherent. We write $\mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)$ for the
57	  category of quasi-coherent sheaves of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras and
58	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra morphisms.
59	\end{definition}

77	% against it.

79	\section{The relative-spectrum scheme}
80	\label{sec:relspec_scheme}
81	
82	The construction is given affine-locally: on every affine open $U = \Spec R \subseteq X$ one
83	sets $\pi^{-1}(U) := \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ regarded as an $R$-algebra, and the local pieces
84	glue compatibly because $\mathcal{A}$ is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
85	$\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)$ for an inclusion
86	$V = \Spec S \subseteq U$ gives an open immersion of the corresponding Specs).
87	
88	\begin{theorem}\leanok
89	[Relative spectrum exists]
90	  \label{thm:relative_spec_exists}
91	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec}
92	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
93	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec)
94	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L381-L405).
95	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
96	  % "In Situation \ref{situation-relative-spec}.
97	  %  There exists a morphism of schemes
98	  %  $$
99	  %  \pi : \underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A}) \longrightarrow S
100	  %  $$
101	  %  with the following properties:
102	  %  \begin{enumerate}
103	  %  \item for every affine open $U \subset S$ there exists an isomorphism
104	  %  $i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \to \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ over $U$, and
105	  %  \item for $U \subset U' \subset S$ affine open the composition
106	  %  $$
107	  %  \xymatrix{
108	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \ar[r]^{i_U^{-1}} &
109	  %  \pi^{-1}(U) \ar[rr]^{inclusion} & &
110	  %  \pi^{-1}(U') \ar[r]^{i_{U'}} &
111	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))
112	  %  }
113	  %  $$
114	  %  is the open immersion of Lemma \ref{lemma-spec-inclusion} above.
115	  %  \end{enumerate}
116	  %  Moreover, $\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})$
117	  %  is unique up to unique isomorphism over $S$."
118	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec);
119	  cf.\ [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(a).}
120	  Let $X$ be a scheme and $\mathcal{A}$ a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra
121	  (Definition~\ref{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}). There exists a scheme
122	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ together with an affine morphism
123	  $\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X$ such that
124	  \begin{enumerate}
125	    \item for every affine open $U \subseteq X$, there is an isomorphism
126	      $i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \xrightarrow{\sim} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ over $U$;
127	    \item for $U \subseteq U' \subseteq X$ both affine open, the composite
128	      $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \xrightarrow{i_U^{-1}} \pi^{-1}(U) \hookrightarrow
129	      \pi^{-1}(U') \xrightarrow{i_{U'}} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$
130	      coincides with the open immersion induced by the restriction
131	      $\mathcal{A}(U') \to \mathcal{A}(U)$.
132	  \end{enumerate}
133	  The pair $(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}), \pi)$ is unique up to unique isomorphism
134	  over $X$.
135	\end{theorem}
136	
137	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Follows immediately from
138	% Lemmas \ref{lemma-relative-glueing},
139	% \ref{lemma-spec-inclusion}, and
140	% \ref{lemma-transitive-spec}.
141	% Uniqueness is stated in the last sentence of
142	% Lemma \ref{lemma-relative-glueing}."
143	\begin{proof}
144	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
145	  By the open-cover gluing principle for schemes (Mathlib
146	  \texttt{Scheme.GlueData} / \texttt{AffineScheme.glueOpens}) it suffices to specify
147	  affine local pieces $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ for each affine open $U \subseteq X$ and
148	  open-immersion transition data
149	  $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \hookrightarrow \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$ for $U \subseteq U'$
150	  satisfying the cocycle condition. The transition morphisms come from the ring maps
151	  $\mathcal{A}(U') \to \mathcal{A}(U)$; quasi-coherence yields the cartesian square
152	  $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) = U \times_{U'} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$, so each transition is an
153	  open immersion. Transitivity (Stacks lemma-transitive-spec) gives the cocycle condition.
154	  Uniqueness of the glued scheme is the standard property of the gluing functor.
155	\end{proof}

157	\begin{theorem}\leanok
158	[Universal property of the relative spectrum]
159	  \label{thm:relative_spec_univ}
160	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty}
161	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras, thm:relative_spec_exists}
162	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as the
163	  % structural consequence ``IsAffineHom (structureMorphism 𝒜)'' rather than
164	  % the natural-bijection statement pinned by this prose. Iter-174+ must

166	  % witness (or equivalent) before any consumer file relies on this theorem.
167	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ continuation: Section ``Relative spectrum as
168	  % a functor'', functor F and Lemma lemma-spec
169	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L427-L466 + L547-L551).
170	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
171	  % "For any $f : T \to S$ the pullback
172	  %  $f^*\mathcal{A}$ is a quasi-coherent sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_T$-algebras.
173	  %  We are going to consider pairs $(f : T \to S, \varphi)$ where
174	  %  $f$ is a morphism of schemes and $\varphi : f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T$
175	  %  is a morphism of $\mathcal{O}_T$-algebras. Note that this is the
176	  %  same as giving a $f^{-1}\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra homomorphism
177	  %  $\varphi : f^{-1}\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T$, see
178	  %  Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjointness-tensor-restrict}.
179	  %  This is also the same as giving an $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra map
180	  %  $\varphi : \mathcal{A} \to f_*\mathcal{O}_T$, see
181	  %  Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjoint-push-pull-modules}.
182	  %  We will use all three ways of thinking about $\varphi$,
183	  %  without further mention. [...]
184	  %  In this way we have defined a functor
185	  %  \begin{eqnarray}
186	  %  \label{equation-spec}
187	  %  F : \Sch^{opp} & \longrightarrow & \textit{Sets} \\
188	  %  T & \longmapsto & F(T) = \{\text{pairs }(f, \varphi) \text{ as above}\}
189	  %  \nonumber
190	  %  \end{eqnarray}
191	  %  [...]
192	  %  The functor $F$ is representable by a scheme."
193	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-spec + section-spec
194	  functor description); [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(b).}
195	  Let $X$ be a scheme and $\mathcal{A}$ a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra.
196	  Then $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ represents the functor
197	  $F : \Sch^{op} \to \mathrm{Set}$,
198	  $T \mapsto F(T) := \{(f, \varphi) : f : T \to X \text{ a morphism},\;
199	  \varphi : f^* \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T \text{ an } \mathcal{O}_T\text{-algebra map}\}.$
200	  Equivalently, for any $X$-scheme $g : T \to X$,
201	  there is a natural bijection
202	  \[
203	    \Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \;\;\cong\;\;
204	    \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_* \mathcal{O}_T)
205	  \]
206	  (using the adjunction between $g_*$ and $g^*$ to convert between
207	  $\mathcal{A} \to g_*\mathcal{O}_T$ and $g^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T$).
208	  The bijection is natural in $T$.
209	\end{theorem}

218	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_exists, thm:relative_spec_affine_base, thm:relative_spec_base_change}
219	  \leanok
220	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-176 placeholder-discharge note.
221	  % The iter-179 Block A refactor replaces the iter-176 placeholder bodies with

230	  % iter-180+). Consumers may now rely on the affineness conclusion, but the
231	  % Yoneda-bijection witness is still deferred.
232	  Following Stacks tag 01LQ (proof of lemma-spec): the functor $F$ is a Zariski sheaf
233	  because morphisms of schemes and $\mathcal{O}$-algebra maps both glue under open covers.
234	  Choose an affine open cover $X = \bigcup U_i$. The subfunctor
235	  $F_i \subseteq F$ of pairs $(f, \varphi)$ with $f(T) \subseteq U_i$ is representable by
236	  $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U_i))$ via the affine-base case

268	  Let $X = \Spec R$ be an affine scheme and let $\mathcal{A}$ be a quasi-coherent
269	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra. Set $A := \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A})$, regarded as an
270	  $R$-algebra. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of $X$-schemes
271	  \[
272	    \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \;\;\cong\;\; \Spec(A).
273	  \]
274	  In particular, on an affine base the relative spectrum reduces to the absolute
275	  spectrum of the global sections.
276	\end{theorem}

334	[Base change of the relative spectrum]
335	  \label{thm:relative_spec_base_change}
336	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.base_change}

363	  Let $g : T \to X$ be a morphism of schemes and let $\mathcal{A}$ be a quasi-coherent

365	  $\mathcal{O}_T$-algebra and there is a canonical isomorphism of $T$-schemes
366	  \[

372	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "A pair $(f' : T \to S', \varphi' : (f')^*\mathcal{A}' \to \mathcal{O}_T)$
373	% is the same as a pair $(f, \varphi : f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T)$
374	% together with a factorization of $f$ as $f = g \circ f'$. Namely with
375	% this notation we have
376	% $(f')^* \mathcal{A}' = (f')^*g^*\mathcal{A} = f^*\mathcal{A}$.
377	% Hence the lemma."

379	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_univ}
380	  \leanok
381	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-176 placeholder-discharge note.
382	  % The iter-179 Lane B closes the existential by witnessing with the named
383	  % helpers ``Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜`` and
384	  % ``Scheme.RelativeSpec.pullback_iso g 𝒜`` (both substantive but with

390	  By Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}, $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ represents
391	  the functor $F$ sending an $X$-scheme $h : S \to X$ to the set of
392	  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra maps $h^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_S$. A morphism
393	  $S \to T \times_X \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ over $T$ is the same as a pair of an
394	  $X$-morphism $S \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ together with a $T$-factorisation
395	  of $S \to X$, i.e.\ a morphism $S \to T$ whose composition with $g$ equals
396	  $S \to X$. Tracing through, this is a $T$-morphism $f' : S \to T$ together with an
397	  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra map $(f')^*(g^*\mathcal{A}) = f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_S$.
398	  This is precisely $F'(S)$, the functor for $(T, g^*\mathcal{A})$, so the canonical map
399	  $T \times_X \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to \underline{\Spec}_T(g^*\mathcal{A})$
400	  is an isomorphism by Yoneda.
401	\end{proof}

403	\section{Functoriality}
404	\label{sec:relspec_functoriality}
405	
406	\begin{theorem}\leanok
407	[Relative spectrum as a functor]
408	  \label{thm:relative_spec_functorial}
409	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.functor}
410	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras, thm:relative_spec_exists, thm:relative_spec_univ}
411	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as a
412	  % bare object-level function ``QcohAlgebra → Over X'' (not a categorical
413	  % ``Functor''), with morphism action + functoriality + the equivalence
414	  % onto affine X-schemes all deferred. Iter-174+ upgrades to the full
415	  % contravariant ``Functor (X.QcohAlgebra)ᵒᵖ (Over X)'' with the universal
416	  % property-induced ``map'' once QcohAlgebra is unpacked.
417	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LR (definition-relative-spec +
418	  % lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec + lemma-spec-properties part (3))
419	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L602-L691).
420	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
421	  % "Let $S$ be a scheme. Let $\mathcal{A}$ be a quasi-coherent sheaf of
422	  %  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras. The {\it relative spectrum of $\mathcal{A}$ over
423	  %  $S$}, or simply the {\it spectrum of $\mathcal{A}$ over $S$} is the scheme
424	  %  constructed in Lemma \ref{lemma-glue-relative-spec} which represents the
425	  %  functor $F$ (\ref{equation-spec}), see
426	  %  Lemma \ref{lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec}.
427	  %  We denote it $\pi : \underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A}) \to S$.
428	  %  The ``universal family'' is a morphism of $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras
429	  %  $$
430	  %  \mathcal{A}
431	  %  \longrightarrow
432	  %  \pi_*\mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})}
433	  %  $$
434	  %  [...]
435	  %  The universal map
436	  %  $$
437	  %  \mathcal{A}
438	  %  \longrightarrow
439	  %  \pi_*\mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})}
440	  %  $$
441	  %  is an isomorphism of $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras."
442	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LR (definition-relative-spec +
443	  lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec + lemma-spec-properties);
444	  [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(b).}
445	  The construction $\mathcal{A} \mapsto \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ assembles into
446	  a contravariant functor
447	  \[
448	    \underline{\Spec}_X : \mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathrm{AffSch}/X
449	  \]
450	  from quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras to affine $X$-schemes: a morphism of
451	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras $\psi : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}$ induces, by the universal
452	  property (Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}) applied to the pair
453	  $(\pi_\mathcal{B}, \psi \circ \pi_\mathcal{B}^*) :
454	  \pi_\mathcal{B}^*\mathcal{A} \to \pi_\mathcal{B}^*\mathcal{B} \to \mathcal{O}_{\Spec\mathcal{B}}$,
455	  an $X$-morphism
456	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\psi) : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$.
457	  Functoriality (identity, composition) is direct from the universal property. The
458	  pushforward $\pi_* \mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})} \cong \mathcal{A}$
459	  exhibits $\underline{\Spec}_X$ as an equivalence between $\mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op}$ and
460	  the full subcategory of affine $X$-schemes, with inverse $f \mapsto f_* \mathcal{O}_Y$
461	  (Stacks lemma-spec-properties part~(3)).
462	\end{theorem}
463	
464	\begin{proof}
465	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_exists, thm:relative_spec_univ, thm:relative_spec_affine_base}
466	  \leanok
467	  Functoriality follows directly from Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}: for
468	  $\psi : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}$, the pair
469	  $(\pi_\mathcal{B} : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to X, \;
470	  \pi_\mathcal{B}^*(\psi))$ defines an $X$-morphism
471	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ via the
472	  representing universal pair. Identity and composition follow from naturality of the
473	  representing bijection in $\mathcal{A}$. For the equivalence claim, $\underline{\Spec}_X$
474	  is fully faithful because the isomorphism
475	  $\pi_* \mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})} \cong \mathcal{A}$
476	  (locally identifying with $\Gamma(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)),\mathcal{O}) = \mathcal{A}(U)$
477	  via Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}) reconstructs $\mathcal{A}$ from
478	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$. Essential surjectivity onto affine $X$-schemes uses
479	  the canonical morphism $X \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\pi_*\mathcal{O}_X)$ of Stacks
480	  lemma-canonical-morphism; on an affine $X$-scheme $\pi : Y \to X$, this canonical
481	  morphism is an isomorphism (affine-locally it is the identity
482	  $\Spec\Gamma(\pi^{-1}(U), \mathcal{O}_Y) \to \Spec\Gamma(\pi^{-1}(U), \mathcal{O}_Y)$).
483	\end{proof}
484	
485	\section{Lean encoding}
486	\label{sec:relspec_lean_encoding}
487	
488	The Lean target file is the new \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean}.
489	The construction proceeds by gluing absolute affine pieces: for each affine open
490	$U \subseteq X$ with $U = \Spec R$ and $A := \mathcal{A}(U)$ regarded as an
491	$R$-algebra, the local piece is $\Spec A$ with structure morphism $\Spec A \to \Spec R = U$
492	induced by the algebra unit $R \to A$. Mathlib provides \texttt{IsAffineOpen.toScheme} for
493	the affine-open inclusion and the absolute \texttt{Scheme.Spec} functor for the local
494	pieces; the transition isomorphisms come from quasi-coherence of $\mathcal{A}$ via
495	\texttt{QuasiCoherent.tensorIso} (or the explicit
496	$\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)$ for $V = \Spec S \subseteq U$).
497	The gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData} (general construction) or
498	the convenience wrapper \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme.glueOpens}; the cocycle
499	condition reduces to Stacks \texttt{lemma-transitive-spec}. The universal property
500	(Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}) is expressed as a
501	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} witness, providing the natural bijection
502	$\Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \cong
503	\Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_*\mathcal{O}_T)$ as a
504	\texttt{Yoneda}-style structure. The base-change isomorphism
505	(Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_base_change}) and functoriality
506	(Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_functorial}) are derived from the universal property by
507	Yoneda. No new core axiom is required; the entire chapter is a definitional/structural
508	construction on top of existing Mathlib affine-scheme infrastructure.
509	
510	\section{Out of scope}
511	\label{sec:relspec_out_of_scope}
512	
513	This chapter packages \emph{only} the relative-spectrum construction and its three core
514	properties. The following downstream constructions live in separate sibling chapters and
515	are explicitly out of scope here:
516	\begin{itemize}
517	  \item \textbf{Relative Picard functor $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$} (A.1.c) --- consumed-by, not
518	    defined-here; its blueprint home is the planned chapter
519	    \texttt{Picard\_RelPicFunctor.tex}.
520	  \item \textbf{Line-bundle pullback on a product $C \times_k T$} (A.1.b) ---
521	    \texttt{Picard\_LineBundlePullback.tex} (planned).
522	  \item \textbf{Connectedness, dimensionality, smoothness of $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$}
523	    --- downstream properties that depend on assumptions on $\mathcal{A}$
524	    (locally finite type, flat, etc.), not unconditional consequences of the construction.
525	  \item \textbf{Identity-component $\Pic^0$ and abelian-variety structure} (A.3) --- a
526	    separate Route~A sub-row, fed by but not constructed in this chapter.
527	\end{itemize}

73	- `depth M = d+1`: there's a regular element `x`, so `depth (M/xM) = d`,
74	  and the LES of `Ext^*(κ, ·)` gives the recursive identity.

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2025 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Directed
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.UniversallyInjective
10	
11	/-!
12	# Relative gluing
13	
14	In this file we show a relative gluing lemma (see https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01LH):
15	If `{Uᵢ}` is a locally directed open cover of `S` and we have a compatible family of `Xᵢ` over `Uᵢ`,
16	the `Xᵢ` glue to a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` such that `Xᵢ ≅ f⁻¹ Uᵢ`.
17	-/
18	
19	@[expose] public section
20	
21	universe u
22	
23	open CategoryTheory Limits
24	
25	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
26	
27	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
28	lemma Scheme.isLocallyDirected_of_equifibered_of_injective {J : Type*} [Category J]
29	    {F G : J ⥤ Scheme.{u}} (s : F ⟶ G) [Quiver.IsThin J] (hs : s.Equifibered)
30	    (H : ∀ {i j} (hij : i ⟶ j), Function.Injective (F.map hij))
31	    [(G ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected] :
32	    (F ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected where
33	  cond {i j k} fi fj xi xj heq := by
34	    simp only [Functor.comp_obj, Scheme.forget_obj, Functor.comp_map, Scheme.forget_map] at heq
35	    obtain ⟨l, fli, flj, x, hi, hj⟩ := (G ⋙ Scheme.forget).exists_map_eq_of_isLocallyDirected fi fj
36	        (s.app i xi) (s.app j xj) <| by
37	      simp only [Functor.comp_obj, forget_obj, Functor.comp_map, forget_map,
38	        ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, TypeCat.Fun.coe_mk]
39	      dsimp at heq
40	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← s.naturality, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, heq,
41	        ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, s.naturality]
42	      simp
43	    use l, fli, flj
44	    let e := (hs fli).isoPullback
45	    obtain ⟨z, h1, h2⟩ := Scheme.Pullback.exists_preimage_pullback xi x hi.symm
46	    refine ⟨e.inv z, ?_, ?_⟩
47	    · simp [← h1, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, e]
48	    · apply H fj
49	      simp only [Functor.comp_obj, forget_obj, Functor.comp_map, forget_map,
50	        ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, TypeCat.Fun.coe_mk, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply,
51	        Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, show flj ≫ fj = fli ≫ fi by subsingleton]
52	      dsimp at heq
53	      simp [e, Functor.map_comp, ← heq, h1]
54	
55	namespace Scheme.Cover
56	
57	variable {S : Scheme.{u}} (𝒰 : S.OpenCover) [Category 𝒰.I₀] [𝒰.LocallyDirected]
58	
59	/--
60	A relative gluing datum over a locally directed cover `𝒰` of `S` is a scheme `Xᵢ` for every
61	`i : 𝒰.I₀` and natural maps `Xᵢ ⟶ Uᵢ` such that for every `i ⟶ j`, the diagram
62	```
63	Xᵢ --> Uᵢ
64	|      |
65	v      v
66	Xⱼ --> Uⱼ
67	```
68	is a pullback square. We bundle this in the form of a functor and an equifibered natural
69	transformation.
70	The `Xᵢ` then glue to a scheme over `S`
71	(see `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.RelativeGluingData.glued`).
72	-/
73	@[stacks 01LH]
74	structure RelativeGluingData where
75	  /-- The schemes `Xᵢ`. -/
76	  functor : 𝒰.I₀ ⥤ Scheme.{u}
77	  /-- The natural maps `Xᵢ ⟶ Uᵢ`. -/
78	  natTrans : functor ⟶ 𝒰.functorOfLocallyDirected
79	  equifibered : natTrans.Equifibered
80	
81	variable {𝒰} (d : RelativeGluingData 𝒰)
82	
83	namespace RelativeGluingData
84	
85	instance {i j : 𝒰.I₀} (hij : i ⟶ j) : IsOpenImmersion (d.functor.map hij) := by
86	  apply MorphismProperty.of_isPullback (d.equifibered hij).flip
87	  infer_instance
88	
89	instance [Quiver.IsThin 𝒰.I₀] : (d.functor ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected := by
90	  apply isLocallyDirected_of_equifibered_of_injective d.natTrans d.equifibered
91	  intro i j hij
92	  exact (d.functor.map hij).injective
93	
94	variable [Small.{u} 𝒰.I₀] [Quiver.IsThin 𝒰.I₀]
95	
96	/--
97	The glued scheme of a relative gluing datum is the colimit over the `Xᵢ`. For the
98	structure map, see `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase` and the isomorphisms
99	with the preimages `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.RelativeGluingData.isPullback_natTrans_ι_toBase`.
100	-/
101	@[stacks 01LH]
102	noncomputable abbrev glued : Scheme.{u} :=
103	  colimit d.functor
104	
105	/-- The cover of the glued `Xᵢ` given by the `Xᵢ`. -/
106	@[simps!]
107	noncomputable def cover : OpenCover d.glued :=
108	  Scheme.IsLocallyDirected.openCover _
109	
110	instance : Category d.cover.I₀ :=
111	  inferInstanceAs <| Category 𝒰.I₀
112	
113	/-- The structure map from the colimit of the `Xᵢ` to `S`. -/
114	noncomputable def toBase : d.glued ⟶ S :=
115	  colimit.desc _
116	    { pt := S
117	      ι := d.natTrans ≫ 𝒰.functorOfLocallyDirectedHomBase }
118	
119	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
120	lemma ι_toBase (i : 𝒰.I₀) :
121	    colimit.ι d.functor i ≫ d.toBase = d.natTrans.app i ≫ 𝒰.f i := by
122	  simp [toBase]
